explicit_text: I was born on July 4, 1907, in a small town in Middle Georgia, where both my parents still live. My father ran a business that started as a blacksmith's shop and later became a modern machine shop. Growing up there shaped much of my early life and values.
implicit_text: I was born on July 4, 1907, in a small town in the central region of Georgia, where both my parents still live. My father ran a business that started as a blacksmith's shop and later became a modern machine shop. Growing up there shaped much of my early life and values.

explicit_text: I attended Mercer University for a year before I stopped to teach school. It was an important step in my education, although I didn't complete my studies there initially. After teaching, I returned to college at the University of Georgia.

explicit_text: After teaching for a year, I went to the University of Georgia in the city where I finished college. There, I enrolled in the Lumpkin Law School and received my Bachelor of Laws degree in July 1929. It was at the University of Georgia that I solidified my goal of becoming a lawyer.

explicit_text: My sister went to college at Bessie Tift College. After finishing there, she trained as a nurse at St. Joseph's Infirmary in Atlanta. Her education at Bessie Tift was the starting point of her nursing career.
implicit_text: My sister went to a women's college in Forsyth, Georgia. After finishing there, she trained as a nurse at a well-known infirmary in Atlanta. Her education at that college was the starting point of her nursing career.

explicit_text: My sister trained to be a nurse at St. Joseph's Infirmary in Atlanta after attending Bessie Tift College. She worked there and also in our hometown and in Gainesville during a terrible storm. Later, she took a special nursing course in Washington, D.C., and now works in a hospital in Lynchburg, Virginia.

explicit_text: At the University of Georgia, I enrolled in the Lumpkin Law School where I had a memorable teacher named Dr. Myers. Despite his strict and peculiar methods, he taught us the principles of law well. I graduated from Lumpkin Law School with my Bachelor of Laws degree in July 1929.

explicit_text: In my legal practice, I have appeared before the Superior Courts of my state after being admitted to practice law. Chamber hearings in these courts are usually informal and held on Saturdays, with a relaxed atmosphere where lawyers and parties can smoke and chew tobacco. The main challenge is getting cases to trial, as continuances often delay proceedings.

explicit_text: I was raised in a Baptist family and joined the church quite young, as all my people were Baptists. I participated in church organizations, taught Sunday school classes, and sang in the choir to please my mother. I even served as a deacon and teacher in my city church, though my busy law practice now limits my attendance.
